title: How to get a police certificate – Côte d’Ivoire - Canada.ca
content:
## How to get a police certificate – Côte d’Ivoire
If you need to give your fingerprints for a police certificate, this isn’t the same as giving your biometrics (fingerprints and photo) for an application.

## Name of the document you need
Extrait de Casier Judiciaire Bulletin No 3

## Police certificate and IRCC requirements
You must provide a police certificate when you apply for:
- some temporary residence programs
Check your application package or instructions for details.

### If you live in Côte d’Ivoire
Citizens: Apply for a certificate online at https://servicepublic.gouv.ci/accueil/detaildemarcheparticulier/1/81/61 . You must provide your original birth certificate. You must also pay the applicable fees of 2500 cfa.
Non-Citizens: If you are not a citizen of Côte d’Ivoire, apply for a certificate at the Department of Foreign Affairs. You will be required to present your residence certificate, your birth certificate and pay the applicable fees (1000 cfa).

### If you live outside Côte d’Ivoire
You must contact the closest Embassy or Consulate of the Republic of Côte d’Ivoire. You will need to provide a document stating the start date and end date of your residency along with the reason of your stay and your address.
